summary: Matched the common fighter passive/tech action code by moving ftCo_800987D0 and the ftCo_Passive Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into a new src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Passive.c file, with a dedicated header and build registration. The PR report shows the new file and all five functions at 100% match. It also refined FtCollisionData by naming the x24 field used as collision data input, and relocated passive prototypes from broader ft_0877.h/ft_08A4.h declarations into ftCo_Passive.h.
what_changed: Deleted the matched assembly for ftCo_800987D0, ftCo_Passive_Anim, ftCo_Passive_IASA, ftCo_Passive_Phys, and ftCo_Passive_Coll from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Passive.c implementing the passive action transition and callbacks in C.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Passive.h with address-commented prototypes for ftCo_800987D0 and the passive callbacks.
Added ftCo_Passive.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk before the remaining ft_08A4 assembly object.
Updated ftCo_DownAttack.c and ftCo_Init.c to include ftCo_Passive.h.
Removed passive-related declarations from ft_0877.h and ft_08A4.h now that a dedicated ftCo_Passive header exists.
Split FtCollisionData padding in src/melee/ft/types.h from u8 x0[0x28] into u8 x0[0x24] plus u32 x24, matching use of fp->ft_data->x4C_collisionData->x24 in ftCo_800987D0.
Removed the sdata2 constants formerly used by the assembly for NULL and 1.0 because the C implementation expresses them as arguments to Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
smart_moves: Isolated the passive action into its own ftCommon source/header pair instead of leaving declarations in generic ft_0877.h and ft_08A4.h buckets.
Used a small static inline helper, inlineA0, around efAsync_Spawn to reproduce the effect-spawn call shape while keeping ftCo_800987D0 readable.
Preserved a MUST_MATCH-only unused stack allocation in inlineA0, with a todo comment, to satisfy codegen without polluting non-matching builds.
Promoted the previously opaque FtCollisionData offset 0x24 into a named struct field once the matched C needed to access it.
Kept simple callbacks minimal: IASA is an empty function, Phys delegates to ft_80084F3C, Coll delegates to ft_80083F88, and Anim exits via ft_8008A2BC when no animation frames remain.
decomp_lessons: When moving a matched action cluster from assembly to C, remove only the matched assembly range and leave following labels such as ftCo_80098928 in place.
Dedicated action headers reduce prototype sprawl in catch-all fighter headers and make later ftCommon work easier to include locally.
MUST_MATCH guards are useful for documenting and containing artifacts such as unused stack bytes required only for matching.
Struct progress can be driven by matched callsites: replacing padding with a field at the exact accessed offset is acceptable when the diff shows concrete use.
For common fighter state callbacks, exact wrappers around existing engine helpers can match cleanly and should not be overcomplicated.
naming_conventions: New common fighter action files follow the ftCo_<Action>.c and ftCo_<Action>.h pattern, here ftCo_Passive.c and ftCo_Passive.h.
Passive callbacks use the established ftCo_Passive_Anim, ftCo_Passive_IASA, ftCo_Passive_Phys, and ftCo_Passive_Coll names.
The transition/setup function remains address-based as ftCo_800987D0, indicating its semantic name was not established in this PR.
The header guard used the address-style identifier GALE01_098430, matching the first named callback address in the new header.
Fighter common object typedefs appear in signatures as ftCo_GObj/Fighter_GObj/HSD_GObj depending on existing local convention and included forward declarations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, 199, Ft_MF_None, NULL, 0, 1, 0) corresponding to motion state 0xC7 and float constants 0.0/1.0 in the removed assembly.
Matched the airborne precondition by checking fp->ground_or_air == GA_Air before calling ftCommon_8007D7FC(fp).
Matched the collision setup call by exposing fp->ft_data->x4C_collisionData->x24 and passing it to ft_800881D8(fp, ..., 127, 64).
Matched the effect spawn by passing gobj, &fp->x60C, 1U, effect id 1053, and fp->parts[FtPart_TopN].joint to efAsync_Spawn through an inline helper.
Used a MUST_MATCH local u8 _[8] = { 0 } in the inline helper to account for an unused stack difference.
Matched simple tail-like action callbacks as thin calls to ft_80084F3C, ft_80083F88, ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, and ft_8008A2BC.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename ftCo_800987D0 to a semantic passive action start/change-state name.
Check whether FtCollisionData::x24 receives a more descriptive name after more collision-data callsites are matched.
Search for other ftCommon action PRs using MUST_MATCH unused stack arrays around efAsync_Spawn or similar vararg/effect helpers.
Review subsequent cleanup of ft_0877.h and ft_08A4.h as more action-specific headers are introduced.
